name: ElmTest.Runner.Console

values.name: runDisplay
values.comment:
Run a list of tests in the IO type from [Max New's Elm IO library](https://github.com/maxsnew/IO/).
Requires this library to work. Results are printed to console once all tests have completed. Exits with
exit code 0 if all tests pass, or with code 1 if any tests fail.

name: ElmTest.Runner.Element

values.name: runDisplay
values.comment: Runs a list of tests and renders the results as an Element

name: ElmTest.Runner.String

values.name: run
values.comment: Undocumented.

values.name: runDisplay
values.comment: Runs a test or test suite. Returns the report as a String

name: ElmTest.Run

values.name: fail
values.comment: Transform a Result into a Bool. True if the result represents a fail, otherwise False

values.name: failedSuites
values.comment: Undocumented.

values.name: failedTests
values.comment: Undocumented.

values.name: pass
values.comment: Transform a Result into a Bool. True if the result represents a pass, otherwise False

values.name: passedSuites
values.comment: Undocumented.

values.name: passedTests
values.comment: Undocumented.

values.name: run
values.comment: Run a test and get a Result

name: ElmTest.Test

unions.name: Test
unions.comment: A test case or a suite.

values.name: defaultTest
values.comment: Automatically determines a name for the created test (use this if you're lazy).

values.name: equals
values.comment: Convenience function for quickly constructing Assert Equals tests.

values.name: suite
values.comment:
Convert a list of `Test`s to a `Suite`. Test suites are used to group tests into
logical units, simplifying the management and running of many tests. The `String` is the
name of the `Suite`.

values.name: test
values.comment: Basic function to create a Test Case

name: ElmTest.Assertion

values.name: assert
values.comment: Basic function to create an Assert True assertion. Delays execution until tests are run.

values.name: assertEqual
values.comment: Basic function to create an Assert Equals assertion, the expected value goes on the left.

values.name: assertNotEqual
values.comment: Basic function to create an Assert Not Equals assertion.

values.name: assertT
values.comment: Basic function to create an Assert True assertion. Delays execution until tests are run.

values.name: assertionList
values.comment:
Given a list of values and another list of expected values,
generate a list of Assert Equal assertions.
values.type: List a -> List a -> List Assertion
